summary: One-line install, in-app upgrade checks, native macOS app bundle, and reliable 1Password CLI integration.

changes.text: Install and upgrade scripts for macOS, Linux, and Windows (`install.sh`, `install.ps1`, `install-local.sh`)
In-app Upgrade pill and Version page check against GitHub releases and `releases/latest.json`
macOS `Vaultify.app` bundle with proper dock/Finder icon and 1Password CLI auth branding (not Terminal)
1Password: CLI path discovery when launched from GUI (Homebrew `op` on minimal PATH), developer settings deep link, and clearer locked/unconnected states
Dev inventory context (JetBrains, VS Code extensions, Eclipse, MCP configs) attached to scan sessions
Archive scan support for `.zip` archives from the dashboard
Export CSV and Export Audit from the dashboard scan menu
Live validation Check button: session ID was read from the wrong App state (stale or missing session error)
Scanner excludes Vaultify's own data dir, test fixtures, and embedded web assets to reduce self-scan noise
macOS app icon set regenerated via `iconutil` (no tiny 16px slots that upscale blurry in system prompts)
Pattern-aware validator routing and validation provider cleanup
